name: Ginger Mascarpone Cheesecake with a Chocolate Crust
recipeInstructions:
Prepare crust (see below).
To make cheesecake:
Preheat the oven to 300°F.
Put the cream cheese and mascarpone in the bowl of an electric mixer. Using the paddle attachment, beat on medium speed until smooth. Continuing to mix, slowly add the sugar. If using a hand mixer, beat with regular beaters as described.
Beat in the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Stir in the grated ginger. Spread the batter over the baked crust.
Place the cheesecake in the oven. Put a pan of hot water on another rack below the cheesecake (steam from the water prevents a crust from forming on top).
Bake the cheesecake for 35 to 60 minutes, until almost set (the center will not be completely firm). When baking is complete, remove the cheesecake and loosen it from the edges by running a knife around the inside edge. Let cool at room temperature for 30 minutes. This helps prevent cracking.
Refrigerate the cheesecake until cold. Unmold by running a knife around the outside edge of the pan and releasing the springform latch.
To serve, cut with a hot dry knife (dip in water briefly, then wipe off).
To make crust:
Combine butter and sugar in an electric mixer bowl. Using the paddle attachment, mix on low speed for 15 seconds. Add flour, cocoa, and salt; continue mixing on low until dough forms (about 5 minutes). If using a hand mixer, beat as described.
Roll the dough into a 10-inch circle and place it on lightly floured baking sheet. Line the bottom and sides of the springform pan (half inch up). Freeze for at least 1 hour.
Bake crust preheated to 225°F for 25 minutes. Set aside to cool.
